message:
security(node): constant-time hash comparisons to mitigate timing attacks in 4 modules

Replace direct equality checks (== / !=) on cryptographic hashes with ct_eq()
constant-time comparisons across security-critical verification paths. Timing
side-channels on hash comparisons can leak information about hash prefixes,
enabling offline brute-force narrowing attacks against Merkle roots and receipt
chain integrity hashes.

Affected modules and what changed:

- control_plane/mmr_proofs.rs: 4 hash comparisons now use ct_eq() for
  inclusion proof and prefix proof root hash validation. Added 2 new tests
  (inclusion_proof_rejects_same_length_tampered_root_hash,
  prefix_proof_rejects_same_length_tampered_prefix_root) with tamper_same_length()
  helper that flips a single byte to verify detection of same-length mutations.

- control_plane/root_pointer.rs: Updated to use fully-qualified
  frankenengine_node::security::constant_time::ct_eq for root hash bootstrap
  validation. Added test bootstrap_rejects_same_length_tampered_root_hash.

- vef/receipt_chain.rs: Both receipt hash and chain hash comparisons now use
  ct_eq(). Added 2 new tests verifying detection of single-byte mutations on
  both hash fields with tamper_same_length_hash() helper.

- supply_chain/transparency_verifier.rs: Leaf hash mismatch detection now uses
  !ct_eq() instead of !=. Added test leaf_mismatch_same_length_rejected.

- lib.rs + main.rs: Added `extern crate self as frankenengine_node;` to enable
  fully-qualified constant-time import paths from within the crate.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
